title: salix × rhaetica
intro: Salix × rhaetica, also known as the Rhäzüns willow, is a hybrid willow plant that is a cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis, and is native to the European Alps. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Salix × rhaetica include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as improving cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Salix × rhaetica include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
